title: Rasmalai

ingredients.title: For the Chenna (Cheese Balls)
items: Lemon juice – 3 tbsp (diluted in 1/4 cup water)

ingredients.title: For the Rabri (Milk Syrup)
items: Saffron strands – 10–15 (soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk)

instruction.title: Step 1: Making Chenna
steps: Boil 2 liters of milk in a heavy-bottom pan.
When it starts to boil, reduce heat and add lemon juice slowly, stirring gently until the milk curdles.
Turn off the heat. Let it sit for 2–3 minutes.
Pour through a muslin cloth to strain the whey. Rinse the chenna with cold water to remove lemon flavor.
Hang the cloth for 30 minutes to remove excess water.
Knead the chenna on a clean surface for 8–10 minutes until smooth and slightly soft.
Divide into small portions and shape into smooth balls, slightly flattened.

instruction.title: Step 2: Preparing Sugar Syrup
steps: In a wide pan, bring 1 cup water + 1 cup sugar + 1/2 tsp cardamom powder to a boil.
Add chenna balls gently, cover, and simmer for 10–12 minutes on low heat.
Remove and let them cool completely.

instruction.title: Step 3: Preparing Rabri
steps: Boil 1 liter of milk in a heavy pan. Reduce to low-medium heat.
Stir continuously and reduce milk to about half.
Add sugar, cardamom, saffron milk, and chopped nuts. Cook 2–3 minutes.
Remove from heat and let it cool to room temperature. Add rose water if desired.

instruction.title: Step 4: Assemble Rasmalai
steps: Gently squeeze chenna balls to remove excess syrup.
Place them in a serving bowl and pour rabri over them.
Chill in the refrigerator for 2–3 hours for flavors to blend.
Garnish with extra saffron strands and nuts. Serve chilled.

chefTips: Use full-fat milk for rich and creamy texture.
Knead chenna thoroughly to make it smooth; it prevents grainy texture.
Keep rabri slightly thick but pourable; it will soak into the chenna.
Saffron soaked in warm milk enhances both flavor and color.
Chill well; Rasmalai tastes best cold.
